mnemonics.title: Emergency Article Sequence Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: Use the sequence 'N-S-F' for National, State (President's Rule), and Financial emergencies: 352 (National), 356 (State/President's Rule), 360 (Financial). Remember: 352 + 4 = 356, and 356 + 4 = 360 (adding 4s).

mnemonics.title: Fundamental Rights Emergency Survival Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: Emergency protection lifeline: '20-21 Never Done'. Articles 20 and 21 can never be suspended during any emergency.

visualAssociations.title: Article 368 as an Amending Engine
visualAssociations.content: Visualize Article 368 as a heavy engine room in Parliament. Only two levers (Special Majority types) control the entire structural track of the Indian Constitution.

visualAssociations.title: The 42nd vs 44th Amendment Seesaw
visualAssociations.content: Picture the 42nd Amendment (1976) as a heavy weight pushing power entirely to the Centre and executive. Picture the 44th Amendment (1978) as a strong counterweight that rebalanced the scale, adding safeguards and protecting Article 21.

stories.title: The Tale of the Unbreakable Core (Basic Structure)
stories.content: Imagine Parliament trying to remodel a historic mansion (the Constitution). They can paint the walls or change the furniture (ordinary amendments under Article 368), but when they tried to tear down the foundational pillars (Kesavananda Bharati case, 1973), the Supreme Court watchman stepped in and said: 'You can renovate, but you cannot touch the Basic Structure!'

articleNumber Tricks.title: Presidential Immunity Trick
articleNumber Tricks.content: Article 361 gives absolute protection to the President and Governors. Think of '361 degrees'—a complete protective circle shielding them from criminal arrest while in office.

articleNumber Tricks.title: Union Directive Penalty Trick
articleNumber Tricks.content: Article 365: If a state defies Union directives for 365 days (a full year), it results in President's Rule under Article 356.

amendment Recall Tricks.title: GST and EWS Digit Hacks
amendment Recall Tricks.content: GST = 101st Amendment (Think: 101 is an auspicious starting gift amount). EWS = 103rd Amendment (Think: 10% quota corresponds to 103).

amendment Recall Tricks.title: Voting Age Reduction Trick
amendment Recall Tricks.content: 61st Amendment lowered voting age from 21 to 18 (1988). Flip the digits: 6 + 1 = 7, or remember voting age dropped when young adults turned 18.

exam Recall Hacks.title: Emergency Approval Time Trap
exam Recall Hacks.content: National Emergency = 1 Month (shorter time, bigger crisis). President's Rule & Financial Emergency = 2 Months (longer time, state/financial scope).

exam Recall Hacks.title: Joint Sitting Exclusion Rule
exam Recall Hacks.content: Never look for a 'Joint Sitting' option for Constitutional Amendment bills or Money bills—they both require separate passage in each House!
